title: Zelensky Removes Ukraine's Top General After Military Leadership Conflict
dek: President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi days after firing Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, following public protests over military leadership tensions. Syrskyi, 60, is replaced by Mykhailo Drapatyi, described as a battle-hardened general.
author: The GoCheckMyNews Desk
key_fact: Syrskyi's removal came just days after the dismissal of Defence Minister Fedorov, whose firing had sparked street protests and revealed a command conflict between the two officials.
bottom_line: The back-to-back removals signal internal friction between modernization and traditional military hierarchy at a critical moment in Ukraine's defence. Watch whether Drapatyi's appointment accelerates military reforms Fedorov was pushing, and whether the command shuffle affects operational continuity in the conflict with Russia.
body: President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi days after firing Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, according to BBC reporting on July 21. The moves follow street protests over military leadership and come after a command conflict between the two officials was confirmed.

Syrskyi, 60, becomes the second major Ukrainian military official removed within days. Fedorov, 35, was terminated first, an action that triggered public protests across the country. Rumours that the two men were in conflict were soon confirmed by reporting from the BBC.

Fedorov has been widely praised for energizing the defence ministry and transforming the armed forces, as well as leading an anti-corruption drive within the military, per BBC reporting. Syrskyi, by contrast, is seen by many in Ukraine as a Soviet-school commander reluctant to implement radical changes in the army.

Zelensky thanked Syrskyi in a social media post on Tuesday for his role in successfully defending Kyiv during the first days of the Russian full-scale invasion in 2022 and in other military campaigns. The president stated in the same post that all decisions will be formalized the following day.

Syrskyi is replaced by Mykhailo Drapatyi, described by the BBC as a popular, battle-hardened general. The specific nature of the strategic disagreements between Fedorov and Syrskyi regarding war strategy, the full scope of public criticism driving the dismissals, and Drapatyi's full military track record remain unclear from public reporting.
